name: Transport logistics — module shipment compliance (EU / US / global)
standard: Abnormal-load transport regulations / ISO 668 container / EU Directive 96/53/EC / US FHWA / STAA
description: Enables OCERP built-in validation rules that check transport packages for each module before factory dispatch. Covers EU (Directive 96/53/EC: 2.55 m standard width, 4 m height, 40 t GVW, 44 t intermodal; abnormal-load thresholds vary by member state — typically >3.0 m width = escort, >4.0 m height = route survey, >44 t = permit), US (Interstate: 8'-6'' / 2.59 m width, 13'-6'' / 4.11 m height, 80 000 lb / 36 287 kg gross; non-divisible loads >12 ft / 3.66 m width need state permits, >16 ft / 4.88 m typically need pilot car + route survey, super-loads >14 ft / 4.27 m height in many states need utility coordination). Validates dimensions vs route envelope, axle weights, escort/pilot car requirement, route survey, permits per jurisdiction crossed, lashing plan, cargo insurance.
